title: transaction — retention windows
description: Transactions + ledger entries are the module's financial-record backbone — 7-year retention default (10 for Enterprise via transaction_extended_retention). No soft-delete on either table — both are append-only. Hard-purge ONLY via PurgeExpiredTransactionDataJob after archiving to compliance-storage. TenantErased triggers archive-to-compliance BEFORE cascade-delete — regulator + tax authority audit trail survives tenant deletion.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gaap — audit-trail retention for reported financial statements
irs / hmrc / eu tax authorities — 7-year statute of limitations for tax audits (some jurisdictions 10 years)
sarbanes-oxley section 802 — 7-year retention for audit workpapers
pci-dss 10.7 — 1-year hot + 6-year archive for audit trails
aml (anti-money-laundering) — 7-year record retention for regulator audits
gdpr Art. 17 §3 — carve-out: legal obligation to retain financial records supersedes erasure right

hot.description: Active + first year for reporting, support queries, dispute investigation

cold.description: Financial-audit retention. Enterprise tenants with transaction_extended_retention entitlement: 3650 (10 years).

purge.trigger: > 7 years past effective_date (10 years for Enterprise)
purge.action: archive to compliance-storage THEN hard delete via PurgeExpiredTransactionDataJob
purge.notes: No soft-delete — the model does NOT use the SoftDeletes trait. Every purge is preceded by an atomic export to compliance-storage keyed by (tenant_id, transaction_number, year). The archive lives at least the tenant's lifetime; a regulator audit can retrieve it years after the tenant erased the source row.

cold.description: Same retention as parent transaction. Cascade via FK. Enterprise: 10 years.

purge.action: hard delete (part of the parent transaction's archive-then-delete flow)
purge.notes: Ledger entries are exported into the parent transaction's compliance archive as embedded rows. When the parent is purged, the entries cascade — but their content survives inside the archive JSONL export.

special_cases.action: ArchiveTransactionsToComplianceOnTenantErased hook fires BEFORE the FK CASCADE — every transaction + its ledger entries + the base_currency-normalized global tenant balance snapshot is exported to compliance-storage keyed by (tenant_id, effective_date). AFTER export verified, the standard FK CASCADE deletes the rows.
special_cases.notes: This is GDPR Art. 17 §3 (financial-record retention carve-out) in practice. The tenant's own data is deleted from the transactional store, but the compliance archive lives on for regulator + tax authority audit. Audit trail rows written during the tenant's lifetime survive per audit module's outlives-source design.

special_cases.action: Both the original AND the reversal transaction retain per their own effective_date — the original is NOT purged early just because it was reversed. Reversal is an accounting mechanism, not a retention mechanism.
special_cases.notes: Regulators need to see BOTH the original and the reversal for the audit story to make sense. A refund audit walks the trail: invoice → invoice_payment transaction → refund → refund_issued transaction (reversal) → adjustment transaction (revenue reversal).

special_cases.action: An unresolved LedgerImbalanceDetected does NOT change retention — the affected transactions are still 7-year retained. New transactions are halted (via tenant.transaction_halt_new flag) but existing transactions remain visible + purge on their normal schedule.
special_cases.notes: This is deliberate — regulators need to see the imbalanced state exactly as it happened, not a curated after-the-fact reconstruction.

special_cases.action: The exchange_rate_to_base + base_amount_cents fields retain co-terminously with the parent transaction. The rate captured at commit is IMMUTABLE — historical reports use the frozen rate, not the current market rate.
special_cases.notes: This is what makes historical multi-currency GL reports stable + reproducible. A 2024 EUR transaction with a locked EUR→USD rate at commit-time reports the same USD equivalent forever.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention.

The compliance/data-subject-rights module orchestrates TenantErased across all modules including this one — the module registers ArchiveTransactionsToComplianceOnTenantErased as a compliance callback that MUST complete before any FK CASCADE.

The compliance/archive module owns the storage backend (S3 with Object Lock + Glacier tiering for cold archive access) — this module just ships JSONL exports keyed by (tenant_id, transaction_number, year).
